overview: Applies an XSLT template to input XML documents, producing a transformed raw document for the next pipeline step. Input documents must be **well-formed XML**. The step takes a specified XSLT template, applies it to the input, and passes the result as a raw document to downstream steps.

xsltPath.help:
Full path to the XSLT template file to apply to input documents.

The template is applied to each input document, and the transformation output replaces the raw document flowing through the pipeline.
xsltPath.values: Absolute filesystem path to an `.xsl` or `.xslt` file.

paramList.help:
Parameters passed to the XSLT template at transformation time. Use `name=value` format with **one parameter per line**.

You can use pipeline variables in parameter values:

| Variable | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `${inputPath}` | Full path of the first input document | |
| `${inputURI}` | URI of the first input document | |
| `${outputPath}` | Full path of the first output document | |
| `${inputPath1}` | Same as `${inputPath}` | |
| `${inputURI1}` | Same as `${inputURI}` | |
| `${outputPath1}` | Same as `${outputPath}` | |
| `${srcLang}` | Language part of source locale | For `de-ch` returns `de` |
| `${trgLang}` | Language part of target locale | For `ja-jp` returns `ja` |
| `${inputPath2}` | Full path of the second input document | |
| `${inputURI2}` | URI of the second input document | |
| `${inputPath3}` | Full path of the third input document | |
| `${inputURI3}` | URI of the third input document | |
paramList.values: One `name=value` pair per line. Values may contain `${variable}` references.
examples: `inputFile=${inputPath2}` — pass the second input document path using a variable

useCustomTransformer.help:
Use a custom XSLT transformer implementation (and optionally a custom XPath engine) instead of the default provided by the JVM.

The default transformer and XPath classes depend on the Java Virtual Machine implementation and version. See the [`TransformerFactory.newInstance()` documentation](http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/javax/xml/transform/TransformerFactory.html#newInstance()) for details on how the factory class lookup works.
notes: The specified factory classes must be available on the Java classpath.

factoryClass.help:
Fully qualified class name of the XSLT `TransformerFactory` implementation to use.

The class must be on the Java classpath at runtime.

xpathClass.help:
Fully qualified class name of a custom `XPathFactory` implementation to use alongside the custom transformer.

This is optional even when using a custom transformer — only needed if the default XPath engine is incompatible with your chosen transformer.

passOnOutput.help: Controls whether the XSLT transformation output is passed as the raw document to the next pipeline step. When disabled, the original input document continues through the pipeline instead of the transformed output.

examples.title: Passing parameters with variables
examples.description: Demonstrates how to pass multiple parameters to an XSLT template, including pipeline variable references for dynamic paths.

examples.title: Using Saxon XSLT processor
examples.description: Configure the step to use Saxon instead of the default JVM XSLT transformer, which is useful for XSLT 2.0/3.0 support.
examples.config:
```yaml
xsltPath: /path/to/transform.xsl
useCustomTransformer: true
factoryClass: net.sf.saxon.TransformerFactoryImpl
xpathClass: net.sf.saxon.xpath.XPathFactoryImpl
```

processingNotes: Input documents **must be well-formed XML** — malformed XML will cause transformation failures.
The step operates on **raw documents** (pre-extraction or post-merge), not on Okapi events.
The `${srcLang}` and `${trgLang}` variables return only the **language part** of the locale (e.g., `de` from `de-ch`), not the full locale tag.
